result:
If I could peel back the layers of the world and rewrite one fundamental rule, I would dissolve the illusion of separation—the belief that we are isolated selves in competition with each other, with nature, and with life itself.

I’d weave into the fabric of human consciousness the visceral, unshakable understanding of *interbeing*: that every breath, every action, every thought ripples through the whole. That a forest, a city, a stranger, a star—all are extensions of the same living system.

This wouldn’t erase individuality or conflict, but it would transform their roots. Greed would feel like stealing from your own body. Hatred would taste like poisoning your own well. Empathy wouldn’t be a moral choice—it would be as natural as breathing.

From this shift, everything else would realign—economies toward regeneration, power structures toward stewardship, education toward wisdom, and technology toward deepening connection rather than distraction.

But this change can’t be imposed—it can only be remembered, piece by piece, in the choices we already make. So perhaps the real question isn’t what *I* would change… but what thread of connection you might choose to strengthen, right where you are.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
